bird.name: South Island robin
bird.summary: The South Island robin (Petroica australis; also known in Māori as the kakaruai) is a sparrow-sized bird found only in New Zealand, where i…

relations.subject: South Island robin
relations.evidence: P.|year=2007|title=Historic and contemporary levels of genetic variation in two New Zealand passerines with different histories of decline|journal=Journal of Evolutionary Biology|volume=20|issue=5|pages=2035–2047|doi=10.1111/j.1420-9101.2007.01362.x|pmid=17714320|doi-access=free}} There are two populations on the east coast of the South Island, and they are slightly more common in the north and west. It has fared slightly better than the Stewart Island robin, which has been relegated to 3 subpopulations, all of which are in Leptospermum swamp scrubland—although this is not their preferred habitat, it is less heavily populated by mammalian predators
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: South Island robin
relations.evidence: P.|year=2007|title=Historic and contemporary levels of genetic variation in two New Zealand passerines with different histories of decline|journal=Journal of Evolutionary Biology|volume=20|issue=5|pages=2035–2047|doi=10.1111/j.1420-9101.2007.01362.x|pmid=17714320|doi-access=free}} There are two populations on the east coast of the South Island, and they are slightly more common in the north and west. It has fared slightly better than the Stewart Island robin, which has been relegated to 3 subpopulations, all of which are in Leptospermum swamp scrubland—although this is not their preferred habitat, it is less heavily populated by mammalian predators
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: South Island robin
relations.evidence: thumb|right|Banded South Island robin at :en:Orokonui Ecosanctuary|Orokonui Ecosanctuary The South Island robin is listed as being of least concern on the IUCN Red List. However, the Stewart Island robin (Petroica australis rakiura) has gone through a couple of bottlenecked populations recently because of deforestation and habitat loss, as well as introduced predators, such as rats, stoats, and feral cats. Because of this sharp decline in the Stewart Island population, several attempts have been made to translocate the Stewart Island robin to other islands nearby where introduced predators do not exist or have been eradicated (see above)
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

However, the Stewart Island robin (Petroica australis rakiura) has gone through a couple of bottlenecked populations recently because of deforestation and habitat loss, as well as introduced predators, such as rats, stoats, and feral cats. Because of this sharp decline in the Stewart Island population, several attempts have been made to translocate the Stewart Island robin to other islands nearby where introduced predators do not exist or have been eradicated (see above)
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

relations.object_summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。
